_help._note: Allowed values + requirements per field (this _help block is ignored by the gate — keep or delete). Run `plumb schema` for the full reference.
_help.task_id: required — ticket/issue/branch id (also the receipt filename)
_help.agent_id: required — which agent or human did the work
_help.intent: required — what + why, plain language — the semantic review reads this
_help.self_modifying: one of: true | false — required — MUST be true if changed_files touch policy.protected_paths; touching one with false is a hard fail; true routes to human review
_help.validation_plan: required — each: { command, reason, required, id?, ci_covered? }
_help.validation_plan[].required: one of: true | false — required — is this check mandatory
_help.validation_plan[].id: optional — optional step id; evidence is matched to it via execution_evidence[].step (robust to a command wording diff)
_help.validation_plan[].ci_covered: one of: true | false — optional — step is corroborated by the ci-evidence gate (real CI run), not manual evidence — may be 'skipped'; also auto-recognized when command matches a policy ci_evidence_checks entry
_help.execution_evidence: required — each: { command, status, output_ref?, skip_reason?, step? }
_help.execution_evidence[].status: one of: passed | failed | skipped — required — required steps must be 'passed' (unless CI-covered); use skip_reason when 'skipped'
_help.execution_evidence[].step: optional — optional id of the validation_plan step this evidence is for (matches validation_plan[].id)
_help.changed_files: required — set by `plumb receipt --write` — don't hand-edit
_help.diff_sha256: required — set by `plumb receipt --write` — never hand-edit
_help.result_summary: required — what changed + how it was verified

intent: Implement issue #22 (marketplace-launch prerequisite): make `plumb init` batteries-included so a repo gets a correct-by-default governed CI in one command, and add `plumb setup-protection` to make the gate a required, blocking check with auto-merge. Extends the scaffolded gate workflow with the ci-evidence poll-wait already wired (so the gate never races CI), adds the DB-migration conventions to AGENTS.md, and adds an auto-detected `rust-sqlx` stack preset (migration-version-collision guard, rust-cache + parallelized CI, policy ci_evidence binding, cargo-chef hint). All presets are opt-in/overridable plain files.

validation_plan.reason: New modules (stack.ts, protection.ts) and the extended scaffold/CLI must typecheck against the existing types.
The compiled output the tests and CLI run from must build cleanly.
Unit tests for the scaffolders (poll-wait + stack presets + idempotency), stack detection, the migration-guard collision logic, and setup-protection (mocked fetch) must pass.
The Action runs the committed prebuilt dist/index.js, which must not drift from src/ (the repo's dist-check job enforces this).

validation_plan.command: node dist/index.js init  (in a scratch Cargo.toml + migrations/ + Dockerfile repo)
validation_plan.reason: End-to-end: init on a rust-sqlx repo produces the gate workflow with poll-wait, the migration guard, rust-cache CI, and a policy with ci_evidence_checks bound to the CI jobs.
The repo's CI jobs corroborate the build and dist freshness on the real runner.

execution_evidence.output_ref: tests 155, pass 155, fail 0 (24 new for stack/protection/scaffold; rest are baseline + #25/#26 tests after rebasing onto master)
dist/index.js rebuilt and committed (in sync with src/)

execution_evidence.command: node dist/index.js init  (in a scratch Cargo.toml + migrations/ + Dockerfile repo)
execution_evidence.output_ref: auto-detected rust-sqlx; created plumbline.yml (poll-wait present), ci.yml, migration-guard.yml, Dockerfile.cargo-chef.example; policy ci_evidence_checks bound. migration-guard exited 1 on a colliding version, 0 on a higher one.

execution_evidence.skip_reason: CI-covered — corroborated by the gate's ci-evidence check against the real GitHub run.

result_summary: Added src/stack.ts (rust-sqlx detection + pure migration-collision logic), src/protection.ts (GitHub-API branch protection + auto-merge, idempotent), extended src/scaffold.ts (poll-wait workflow, stack presets, policy binding) and src/cli.ts (init --stack/--no-stack/--protect, setup-protection, migration-guard). New templates: gate workflow with ci-evidence poll-wait, rust-sqlx CI (rust-cache + parallelized), migration-guard workflow, cargo-chef Dockerfile hint; AGENTS.md migration conventions. README now leads with the two-command onboarding. Rebased onto master (which now carries #25/#26 provider + cost controls); both feature sets coexist. Verified via typecheck + build + 155 passing unit tests (24 new) + an end-to-end init smoke test on a scratch rust-sqlx repo and a live migration-guard collision/pass run; dist bundle regenerated and byte-current against dist-check. self_modifying:true — touches the gate workflow templates and .plumbline conventions, so REVIEW is expected.
